comment_content: Old, repetitive, and with some mistakes in the slides. The slides are not enough to fully understand the concepts. You can search for them or attend the class to fully understand them.
comment_teaching: I suggest attending the lectures as well. The instructor takes attendance sometimes (maybe two to three times in the whole semester). I'm not sure if they matter. 10% of each session is useful; the rest is not.
comment_workload:
2 homeworks which were easy and 1 bonus homework which was easier.
No programming assignments.
The midterm and finals need some studying but they're also openbook (you can bring the slides to the exam).
comment_grading: Not bad in grading. It's easy to get B+ and with a good understanding of the basics you can get an A.

comment_content: The content is not too much or too less. It does not cover web security but some other most common computer network basis. Application layer, transport layer, network layer, link layer and physic layer.
comment_teaching: The professor is very nice and gives a lot of examples to help students understand in class.
comment_workload: There are 2 mandatory assignments to gain 30% scores. The third assignment is optional if you are happy with your first two assignments.
comment_grading: The grading is overall good. It seems everyone could achieve at least B, as long as no cheating.

comment_content:
1.Introduction
Introduction to Networking
Network Architectures

2.Application Layer
Application Layer Protocols: HTTP, FTP, SMTP, DNS, CDN, Streaming
P2P Networks and Applications

3.Transport Layer
The TCP/IP and Internet Architecture
Transport Protocols: TCP and UDP
Reliable data transfer
------------------------------------ ↑ midterm
TCP Congestion Control

4.Network Layer: Data path and Control
Network Protocol: IP
Switch architecture, Packet scheduling and buffer management
Network Routing and Routing Algorithms
Classless Inter-Domain Routing (CIDR)
IP Version 6 (IPv6)

5.Link Layer and Local Area Networks
Link Layer services
Multiple Access Protocols: Aloha/Ethernet
Bridges and Switches
comment_teaching: I think it's good. The lessons are clear and interesting, and the content isn't difficult to understand.And even if you can't attend the lectures for some reason, reviewing the slides before the exam will be enough to help you easily learn most of the content.
comment_workload:
There are three assignments in total, each with a relatively small number of questions, which is very manageable. Assignments account for 30% of the final grade. Only the two highest-scoring assignments are counted, meaning one assignment can be skipped. Overall, the workload on assignments is minimal.
If you never attend classes and haven't studied any related content, you can still prepare for the exam by reviewing your homework and slides before the exam, and the workload remains not heavy
comment_grading:
I believe that if you spend a normal amount of time preparing before the exam (or you master the basic content of the slides and assignments), it's difficult to get a score significantly lower than average. However, getting a high score isn't easy either.

Midterm exam is obviously more difficult than final exam. But getting a score above 80 on the final exam isn't always easy. This depends on luck and the grading criteria.

comment_content:
Typical contents about top-down computer network.
The teaching material is the classic top-down book.
However, I dont think you can learn something new if you used to take UG computer network course, especially in mainland china which might cover far more contents.
comment_teaching:
BBB teachs much more better than instructor at my undergraduate host institution.

And also, he teachs much more better than most of my UG CSE cources' instructors.

And the cost is the progess of the course is too slow.
comment_workload: Two assignments. Its a very low workload. And the content of the course is very simple. BBB will teach much more less content even than a UG computer network course.
comment_grading: If you want to spend some time on reviewing, you can get a good score at the final. BUT, more PhD students do not have time to review.

comment_content: 就是自顶而下课本的5个layer，不过最后没有讲完，只讲到了第六章。
comment_teaching: 老师讲课很认真
comment_workload: 不错的工作量，2个HW
comment_grading: 期末考了链路算法，DV算法，CRC，ARP，第一道大题考的略杂有点说不清楚考了啥，总体上难度很适中，但期中考试的强度确实不低，需要提前认真复习。会出课本上的题目，想拿高分把课后题刷一遍，再看看往年卷(可早至2003年)就没什么问题。要看课本，光看PPT绝对不行。但是期末考乱改分，有个TA连数都算不对，我说他改错了他也不给改，说已经过了平均分不用担心

comment_content: stuff hasn't been changed for 15 years, except the powerpoint was cut down by the publisher, and he will go back and teach u those stuff very in-depth
comment_teaching:
good, very very good, it's better than the one you can find online which only talk about how stuff works, but he tries to explain why stuff are designed like this from a kinda researcher's (?) perspective (because he was indeed in that era when networking was being designed and stuff)

but lmao
he tried to only release the lecturer recordings if there was sufficient attendance
then he tried to only release recordings just before the midterm, like 1-2 days
then after the midterm the attendance was so low (like only 5 people attended) that he just gave up and released all recordings
i feel bad lol
comment_workload: meh
comment_grading:
One of the midterm questions was like, you want to send 9 segments with go back n and selective repeat, no lost acks, window size 3, but a loss event happens every 5 packets, then how many segments do you need to send all 9 of them for each algo, draw a time space diagram

He made the midterms very tough

Final easier, but a lot of traps
Like ARP algorithm is link layer algorithm, the way each switch knows about where each node came from, how distance vector are not passed between nodes which don't have a direct edge (consider a 4 node graph)

comment_content:
Basically, just finish some after-class homework. The content is a bit similar to the undergraduate course, and it is not difficult.

Principles of computer network architectures and communication protocols; the OSI
reference model; switching and multiplexing techniques; data link, network, transport and
application layers; LAN and medium access protocols; network programming.
Prerequisite(s): COMP 3511; Exclusion(s): COMP 5621, ELEC 3120, ISOM 3180;
Background: Probability and statistics
comment_teaching: The content of the lecture is average, but fortunately, the assessment is not strict.
comment_workload: The overall pressure is not big, or even very small, if you just want to pass this course.
comment_grading: The score is also good. Most of them can be passed. Finish your homework.

comment_content: The content is about the basic knowledge of network, even if you have never learnt about the network.
comment_teaching: The teaching is clear with many examples and comparisons. It's easy to understand. The instructor is nice.
comment_workload: There is no coding project, just assignments with some TF, selection, and 1-2 short-answer questions. The quiz is also in the format of time-limited assignment. The workload is not heavy.
comment_grading: The grading is normal. I got the score above the average in the assignments, quiz and exams, and I got a B+.

comment_content: Include application layer, transport layer, network layer and link layer, ranging from small local area networks (LANs) to vast global networks like the Internet, enabling seamless communication and collaboration. The link layer was not introduced in class.
comment_teaching: Very good, as it is detailed and demonstrates extensive thinking and depth of understanding. It effectively conveys a thorough analysis and reflects a strong grasp of the subject matter.
comment_workload: Only three assignments without presentation or project. The assignments were very easy and it took about one hour to complete each.
comment_grading: The final exam was easy while the middle-term was difficult, but the final grade was acceptable.

comment_content: 整体上课一般，可能需要课后自己多学习；上课内容整体简单，但是期中和期末考试的内容beyond the content in class.
comment_teaching: 老师上课比较慢，这次都没上完，最后给video自己看
comment_workload: 比较小，但是考试很难
comment_grading: 高分别想，但是过B，把要求都做了基本都能拿到

comment_content: the content was quite interesting and easy to follow. it is okay not to know anything about networks and take this course, you will start with the very basics. The professor will ask in the beginning of the semester if you want to have more homework instead of a midterm. In my case, we had a midterm, a few small homeworks, and a final.
comment_teaching: The professor records all lectures and releases them for later review. His explanations are quite clear. If you have questions you better ask during the class. Questions on canvas were ignored lol.
comment_workload: Questions covered in the homework and quizzes are not repeated in the exams. Homework is generally easy and does not take long to finish. There were 3 homework assignments on Canvas in total. The shark web exercises were optional and recommended for the exams. If you come to lectures you don't need to study extra to understand what's going on. Just look up for few example questions from the book to pass the quizzes.
comment_grading: The Midterm was very hard, but the final was easy. Since the homeworks and final were weighted more, he gave generous grading.

comment_content: The course covered basic concepts of computer networking, which were quite similar to what I learned during my undergraduate studies. For students already familiar with networking fundamentals, the content was easy to grasp. However, it could have benefited from more advanced topics to challenge students further.
comment_teaching: The instructor's teaching was adequate, though I did not attend many sessions due to the introductory nature of the material. The lectures were clear but lacked the depth that would engage students more interested in exploring advanced aspects of networking.
comment_workload: The workload was light, mainly consisting of homework assignments and an exam. The assignments were not particularly difficult and didn’t require extensive effort, making it manageable for students balancing multiple courses. Overall, the course stress level was low.
comment_grading: The grading system was generally fair, but some of the questions were overly strict, especially when dealing with less common concepts. For example, missing a small detail like multiplying by two for the full-duplex and half-duplex concepts could result in significant point deductions. This felt a bit unreasonable, as it emphasized memorization over understanding.

comment_content:
Chapter 1:Introduction to Computer Networking
Chapter 2:The Applications Layer (notes updates)
Chapter 3: The Transport Layer
Chapter 4:The Network Layer
Chapter 5: The Link Laver and Local Area Networks
comment_teaching: The professor's teaching is very good, and it's very easy to follow in the class.
comment_workload: The workload is low,  the homeworks and exams are not very difficult, so you do not need to spend too much time on them.
comment_grading: The grading depends on assignments, mid-term exam, and final exam. I think the grading is not bad and It is not very difficult to get a high score.

comment_content:
Chapter 1: Introduction to Computer Networking
Chapter 2: The Applications Layer (notes updates)
Chapter 3: The Transport Layer
Chapter 4: The Network Layer
Chapter 5: The Link Layer and Local Area Networks

Book: James F. Kurose and Keith W. Ross Computer Networks: A Top Down Approach Featuring Internet, 6th, 7th or 8th Edition, Pearson (the latter is preferred)
comment_teaching:
Easy to understand.
The professor took lots of notes in the class, and this is the key to getting a good score in the exam.
comment_workload:
The overall workload is very good.
Homework is very easy to finish (by chatGPT?).
The exam is open-note in this semester.

comment_content: The content includes multiple aspects of networks, like Internet architecture， all kinds of protocols, and methods to facilitate large-scale networks, etc
comment_teaching: The professor exhibits exceptional prowess in teaching, making complex topics both accessible and engaging. His lectures are a blend of clarity and enthusiasm, where difficult concepts are broken down into understandable segments. He uses a variety of teaching methods, including interactive discussions, real-world examples, and thought-provoking questions, to stimulate deep thinking and foster a dynamic learning environment.
comment_workload: The workload for the course itself wasn't heavy, which is a cause for concern, as a significant portion of the exam content was not covered in the course materials. This necessitated gathering additional resources and studying on my own, thereby substantially increasing the workload outside of class.
comment_grading: The grades were not particularly satisfying. I spent quite a lot of time reviewing the course materials, but the exam content turned out to be significantly more challenging than what was described in class. Although the majority of students are likely to receive a B+, the overall grading seems to be on the lower side, especially considering that the assignments were closely related to the course materials.

comment_content: The course focuses on the architecture of the computer network, in a top-down perspective, including application layer, transport layer, network layer and link layer (physical layer is out of scope of the course).
comment_teaching: BENSAOU, Brahim taught very well, with fruitful examples to explain nearly each hard-to-understand concept in the course.
comment_workload: The workload of the course is not high. We only have three homework assignments, a midterm exam and a final exam. Each homework assignment is easy and will not spend too much time.
comment_grading: My grade is slightly higher than the mean score of the entire class. And my final GPA is B+. I think the score is OK for me.

comment_content: The content covers the basics of computer network. It is suitable for people who has not taken network course before. No complex algorithm or math.
comment_teaching:
The teacher's english is easy to understand. Lecture covers content of the powerpoint. Professor adds some additional hand-written notes during the lecture.

No attendance requirement. But the recording will only be released if there are more than half of the students attend the lecture.
comment_workload:
The workload is light. Two mandatory homework and one optional. One mid-term and one final. That's all.

Both midterm and final are open book. The note can only be taken from the powerpoint or in-class note.
comment_grading:
Most of the students can get B+. I was a bit below mean in both midterm and final but I still received B+ as final grade.

btw. A previous comment says that prof will not give B+ to non-phd student. This is not true. I am a non-phd student and I have asked professor about this. He said the grade was only based on your homework and exam results.

comment_content: It is boring for people who are not studying network, and even more boring for people who are studying network. It is not as in-depth as undergraduate courses in other schools.
comment_teaching: Generally speaking, it's OK. The teacher will record the screen, but it may be due to the teaching content itself, which is quite boring.
comment_workload: 3 homework (one is optional), one mid-term, and one final. The exams are open-book. Overall, it is ok for me.
comment_grading: All my classmates got B+, but we are actually quite a bit higher than the mean.

comment_content: In this class, we use the textbook Computer Networking: A Top-Down Approach by James F·Kurose / Keith W. Ross. The class mainly contains the following chapters: Introduction, The Applications Layer, The Transport Layer, The Network Layer, and The Link Layer and Local Area Networks.
comment_teaching: The teaching of the professor is very good and he is a nice person. He will provide some of the class recordings.
comment_workload: The workload is okay, which mainly comes from 3 homework and two exams.
comment_grading: Grading is also okay. The tutors will give enough feedback for the homeworks.

comment_content: The content is good for layman.
comment_teaching: Prof. BB is teaching in a good way that is easy to unsderstand
comment_workload: Workload is ok.
comment_grading: Grading is ok though I study hard. But professor is very fair to everyone. In that semester, our TA also join the class as a student. He can access some material on canvas that we can not. To be fair to everyone, professor released the hidden material for us including the final exam in the last year which is very useful material for us to prepare the exam.

comment_content: The content is well organized and easy to understand, but we can still learn a lot from this coruse. Really recommend this course!
comment_teaching: The teacher is nice and speak very confortable English. No attendance requres.
comment_workload: There are 3 assignments in total. However, we are only required to  select two of them to complete. Mid and Final exams exists and we need to pass. No projects. I think the workload is very nice especially in PG course.
comment_grading: Good for post graduate students. Most of the student can pass the course.

comment_content: The course content is similar to the corresponding UG course, so you may find it almost impossible to learn something advanced. However, the course does cover the basics of top-down hierarchical network architecture.
comment_teaching: The professor's elaboration is clear and easy to understand, but some may say it is bland.
comment_workload: The workload is reasonable, including four assignments(each costs less than two hours to finish), one mid-term exam, and one final exam.
comment_grading: The grading is okay, and it is not hard to get a B+ as long as you finish all the assignments and get some points in exams.

comment_content: The course's content includes the basics of computer networking - in a top-down approach. I believe the course successfully covers the basics that a CS student should know. The contents are basically at an "undergrad course" level. It would be better if more advanced topics of networking could be covered.
comment_teaching: Prof. BB's teaching is informative and the delivery is interesting.
comment_workload: The workload is appropriate. Two (simple) homework, midterm and final.
comment_grading: Hmm as a newcomer to ust, I am not very sure how students are finally graded. I ranked top 10% in the midterm and top 25% in the final, above the mean for each homework, and got A-.

comment_content: Though I do not enroll in a proper UG network class, I think the content is almost the same as a UG course, without more advanced stuffs. So if you have taken a UG network course, this class can be not so useful, and using this class to easily satisfy credit needs seem not very easy (check the grading section). But if you have not learned network at all, this course can be some simple start.
comment_teaching: The teaching is fine. I understand most things, though I still need to check the slides and textbook sometimes.
comment_workload: Workload is really light: 3 assignments with the last one not count towards final grades, 2 quizzes and 1 final exam. I finish each assignment in roughly 2-3 hours.
comment_grading: Grading is fine: most people get passed (B+ or above for PG student), but getting A+ seems extremely difficult. In fact even getting A requires some effort. I get A- personally with a very low quiz score.

comment_content: I think the major content is the same as what I studied in ug stage. When I was taking the course, I tended not to take the lecture but look at the slides.
comment_teaching: The teaching is just ok. BB will follow the textbook. His illustration is good.
comment_workload: Just need to spend time on the 4 homeworks.
comment_grading:
The grading is very good. As long as you finish the homework and got some points in exam, you can get B+.
For exam, a suggesting is to finish all the problem sets in the Computer Network: Top down approach. It will be enough for the final exam.

comment_content: The class is generally easy. Almost the same content as ug course and the material hasn't been changed for 30 years. Not useful, but understand it is not a bad thing.
comment_teaching: The lecturer always makes students sleepy. The things taught are pretty aged so no need be focus too much. But you still need to focus because BB will highlight some questions for homework and quizes. Some questions that may appear in quizes and finals may not appear in the slides but got mentioned in the class
comment_workload:
3 homeworks, each takes around 2 hours.
2 quizes and 1 final, the questions are not hard but pretty confusing. For instance, you don't know what the questions really want you to answer.
comment_grading: Good. Most students get B+ (65%). Around 5/80 students get failed for this course. But it's not easy to get good grade such as A+, only 2 students get this mark. For PG students, this is not satisfactory unless you just want a B+ to pass. In addition, BB will not give B+ to those non-phd students "because they don't need"

comment_content: I have taken this course in UG, I think this course is the same as what I took. This course will help the learners to understand the basic concepts of computer networks and network protocol.
comment_teaching:
the professor is nice and humorous;
there's more handwriting which is hard to recognize.
comment_workload: not sure
comment_grading:
The grading includes 4 assignments and the mid/final exam.
The professor declares if you are not good at English, he won't take it into consideration, as long as he knows if you are in the scope of the right answer.

comment_content: The content is about TCP/IP model. If you have studied it as a UG, then this course barely cover new stuff for you.
comment_teaching: The delivery is smooth and intriguing. The professor clarified some concepts not clear to me when I was a UG.
comment_workload: Balanced workload. You will have 3 or 4 assignments (I don't remember the exact number). The questions could be answered according to the lectures.
comment_grading: Grading is fare and no need to appeal. It has specific standards to balance the grading of assignments and exams. The midterm and final-term are pretty similar each year.

comment_content: The content is about TCP/IP model. If you have studied it as a UG, then this course barely cover new stuff for you.
comment_teaching: The delivery is smooth and intriguing. The professor clarified some concepts not clear to me when I was a UG.
comment_workload: Balanced workload. You will have 3 or 4 assignments (I don't remember the exact number). The questions could be answered according to the lectures.
comment_grading: Grading is fare and no need to appeal. It has specific standards to balance the grading of assignments and exams. The midterm and final-term are pretty similar each year.

comment_content: Covers 5 different layers of network protocols (Physical, Link, Network, Transport, Application) in top-down approach. ( Application -> Physical).
comment_teaching: (Super) Great at teaching but his speech may be too fast to follow. (Recommend to voice record his lecture)
comment_workload: The workload is super manageable as there are max 3 assignments that are easy to finish.
comment_grading: 2 Assignments, Open-book Mid Term and Open-book final (Online Final examinations). I think the grading is generous and the questions for the assignments are quite easy and most of the time you will get close to 100/100 marks. The midterm/final exam questions are derived from the textbook so recommend solving some of the questions from the textbook problem sets.

comment_content: It includes the basic knowledge of computer network, according to the textbook of Computer Network: A Top-Down Approach (chapters 1-6).
comment_teaching: Great Teaching. The teacher is good at explaining things in detail.
comment_workload: The workload is not high. You would have 3 assignments. And the labs are very easy by using Wireshark. Also open book and open note exams.
comment_grading: Easy to get high grade. As it is the PG's course, I think it may be easier to get high grade than UG's computer network course. And the two exams (mid-term and final-term) are not hard since you can go through the past paper and be familiar with these questions.

comment_content: Standard networking stuff, nothing too exciting. May be too easy if you have some previous experience working with network.
comment_teaching: Great teaching. It is fun in BB's lecture. He is presenting the material with animated power points which allows better understanding. Also very kind to answer questions, in great detail, either during lecture or email.
comment_workload: Light workload. There are 3 assignments asking questions about the content and some wireshark tasks. Easy enough to finish within an hour. Also open book and open note exam.
comment_grading: Easy A range. As it is a PG course it is graded pretty nice. As the exam ask for more thinking question and less memorizing questions, you need to understand the material.

comment_content: Maybe you have taken Computer Networks course during the undergraduate period, this PG course helps you get deeper understanding of Computer Network structure, by a top-down approaches.
comment_teaching: BB is very good at teaching and like to offer latest information about some protocol stack and encourage you to read RFC documents yourselves. If you like to work on protocols, you shouldn't miss one lesson.
comment_workload: 3 assignments & midterm. No programming assignments. No presentations or any essays.
comment_grading: If you works hard enough on homework and textbook exercises, you should easily get fair enough grade even if you fail on either midterm or final.

comment_content: This course covers basic network content according to the textbook top-to-down, mainly includes the first 6 chapter.
comment_teaching: BB is very interesting and trends to explain the details of the basic process in network. Attendance of the course is not required
comment_workload: Two exams, about 4 assignments, including several online labs, no coding projects. The homework and exam are mainly from the exercise in the textbook.
comment_grading: Midterm and final exams are easy to get good grades. If you attend the course and do homework by yourself, it is not hard to get a good grade in final.
